# Welcome {.unnumbered}

Welcome to SKL401 — Introduction to Python. This is a digital seminar designed as a fundamental skills module for the *Master in Economics and Business Administration* (MØA) at NHH. The course covers everything from installation and basic programming to data wrangling, visualization, and reporting. The content has been selected to serve as a prerequisite for other courses at NHH that use Python as their programming language. The goal is that by the end of this course you should feel comfortable enough with Python to use it as an analytical tool for basic data handling and visualization in other courses.

Python is a free, open-source programming language known for its clarity and versatility. First released in 1991, it has grown into one of the most widely used programming languages in the world, with particularly strong adoption in data science, economics, and finance. Its strength lies in a rich ecosystem of packages that extend the language to almost any analytical task. Throughout this course we will explore some of the most central and user-friendly packages for working with, analysing, and visualising data such as pandas, matplotlib, and statsmodels.

<!-- Video 1.1: Welcome -->
*Video will be embedded here.*


## Course overview

The course is organized into five topics:

1. **Getting Started and Working Effectively** — Installing Python, navigating files, writing scripts, and understanding basic data structures.
2. **Programming Fundamentals** — Logical operators, control flow, loops, and writing your own functions.
3. **Data Wrangling** — Importing, cleaning, and transforming data with pandas.
4. **Graphics** — Building clear and informative visualizations with matplotlib.
5. **Analysis, Reporting, and Becoming Self-Sufficient** — Linear regression, exporting results, working with Jupyter Notebooks, debugging, and using AI responsibly.

## How the course works

The material is presented as short video lessons, each covering a single topic. The videos are followed by a series of programming problems of varying difficulty, and it is *crucial* that you spend time on these problems in order to develop muscle memory and to get a sense of where you are in your own learning process. The course material is self-contained, which means that you can start learning **whenever and wherever you want.**

## Using Artificial Intelligence while learning

There is an important difference between *learning* to program and programming efficiently as a professional. Once you know a language well, AI tools can be a powerful way to write code faster and handle routine work. But while you are still learning, the picture is more complicated. The struggle of working through a problem yourself — including making mistakes and figuring out why — is precisely what builds lasting skill. Offloading that struggle to AI short-circuits the process.

That said, AI can be a genuinely useful learning companion when used with care. During this course, you should follow these rules:

**✅ Use AI to:**

- 💬 Explain an error message you don't understand
- 🔍 Clarify a concept that hasn't clicked after reading the material
- 🤔 Talk through *why* your approach isn't working, without just asking for the solution
- ✔️ Check your understanding after you've already solved a problem yourself

**🚫 Avoid using AI to:**

- 💻 Write code on your behalf before you've made a serious attempt
- 📝 Produce solutions to the practice problems — these exist for *your* benefit
- 📖 Substitute for reading documentation or course material

Note that all students at NHH have access to a privacy-friendly AI assistant through [Sikt KI](https://ki.sikt.no/en), which you are welcome to use as a learning companion in this spirit.

## Where to get up-to-date information

This web-page only contains the contents of the course, in the form of video lessons and accompanying problem sets. For semester-specific information such as announcements, dates and locations for physical learning sessions, you should consult the course's Canvas page, to which you will gain access once you have completed your semester registration.

For exam dates, see the [official schedule information from NHH](https://www.nhh.no/en/for-students/schedule/).

::: {.callout-tip}
## Found a mistake?
If you spot any errors or bugs on this page — in the text, the code, or the exercises —
please let me know at [isabel.hovdahl@nhh.no](mailto:isabel.hovdahl@nhh.no). Your
feedback helps make the course better for everyone.
:::
